feed.title: SAP Community - ABAP Cloud
feed.subtitle: ABAP Cloud blog posts in SAP Community

entry.title: CDS : How to define analytical query using cell definition
entry.content:
This blog is about how to use cell definition in CDS analytical query as like in BW query. Lets start with brief overview about two structures.

 

Two Structures

The structure forms the foundational layout of the axes (rows or columns) in a data table, determining how information is organized and presented. The arrangement of the structure affects the order and quantity of key figures or characteristics displayed in the rows and columns of a query or report. More information can be found in  CDS : How to define analytical query using two str... - SAP Community

Cell Definition

A cell is the intersection between two structural components. Each cell in grid is defined by an element of the measures-structure and one of the characteristic-structure. 

Exception cell: When implicit cell definition overwritten by an explicit definition, cell is called as exception cell. It can be defined using formula or selection. Formulas for a cell must only use operands which are cells. 

Help cell: It does not have any direct connection with the corresponding structural elements. It is not embedded in the grid and not displayed, but can be used in formulas of an exception cell. 

In BW modelling tool query designer, In 'Cell' tab we can specify the cell definition.

In the above screenshot marked,

Red    : Exception cell 

Green : Formula to calculate the value for the exception cell

Blue   : Help cell

In analytical query to define the Exception cell, following annotation is used.

 

@AnalyticsDetails.query: {

  isCell: true,

  cellReference: {

    measureStructureElement: 'seatsocc',

    characteristicStructureElement: 'yearPlaceholder'

  }

}

 

A cell is specified with “@AnalyticsDetails.query.isCell: true”,

  • If it should overwrite a cell of the grid, then the coordinates can be specified with @AnalyticsDetails.query.cellReference (Exception Cell)
  • Otherwise, the element is invisible and must be hidden Consumption.hidden:true. (Help Cell)

A value of a cell in the grid can be obtained with CDS function - GET_CELL_REFERENCE_VALUE, which can be used in formula of an exception or help cell. 

Example

In the following example, 'exceptionCellRatioPercent' is Exception cell, cellReference defines exact coordinate of this cell. 'helpCellratio' is a helper cell, it does not have cellReference, it is not part of grid and has Consumption.hidden:true. It is used in formula of an exception cell 'exceptionCellRatioPercent' to calculate percentage of occupied seats.

 

@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_ALLOWED
@EndUserText.label: 'Query with structure and cell definition'
@ObjectModel.modelingPattern: #ANALYTICAL_QUERY
@ObjectModel.supportedCapabilities: [#ANALYTICAL_QUERY]
// CDS Query like the normal query ZLR_2STRUCT_CELL_CAL
define transient view entity ZLR_2STRUCT_CELL_REF
provider contract analytical_query
as projection on ZOQ_FLIGHT
{

//Elements of the Key Figure Structure
@AnalyticsDetails.query.axis: #ROWS
@EndUserText.label: 'Max Seats'
seatsmax,

@AnalyticsDetails.query.axis: #ROWS
@EndUserText.label: 'Occupied Seats'
seatsocc,


//Elements of the Characteristic Structure
@AnalyticsDetails.query.axis: #COLUMNS
@EndUserText.label: '2020'
@AnalyticsDetails.query.onCharacteristicStructure: true
case when flyear = '2020' then 1 else null end as year2020,

@AnalyticsDetails.query.axis: #COLUMNS
@EndUserText.label: 'Occupied %'
@AnalyticsDetails.query.onCharacteristicStructure: true
case when flyear = '0000' then 1 else null end as yearPlaceholder,


abap.unit'%' as unitPercent,

//Help cell - used in formula of exception cell
@Aggregation.default: #FORMULA
@AnalyticsDetails.query: {
  isCell: true  
}
@Consumption.hidden: true
// scalar function with use of cell refernce
ratio_of( portion => GET_CELL_REFERENCE_VALUE(measure_struc_element => $projection.seatsocc, characteristic_struc_element => $projection.year2020 ),
total   => GET_CELL_REFERENCE_VALUE(measure_struc_element => $projection.seatsmax, characteristic_struc_element => $projection.year2020) )  as helpCellratio,


// Exception cell 
@Aggregation.default: #FORMULA
@AnalyticsDetails.query: {
  isCell: true,
  cellReference: {
    measureStructureElement: 'seatsocc',
    characteristicStructureElement: 'yearPlaceholder'
  
  }
}
@AnalyticsDetails.query.decimals: 2
@Semantics.quantity.unitOfMeasure: 'unitPercent'
$projection.helpCellratio * 100 as exceptionCellRatioPercent

 
}

 

Output

More information can be found Cell, Exception Cell and Help Cell | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: Customer & Partner Roundtable for SAP BTP ABAP Environment #21
entry.content:
👉 The call for contributions for one of the upcoming roundtables is open! 

 

If you want to show a demo or share a use case scenario for SAP BTP ABAP Environment send us an email and we will get back to you.
 

Introduction

 

As SAP BTP ABAP environment (aka Steampunk) and ABAP Cloud became more popular inside and outside of SAP, there is a high demand for rolling out the latest product news and updates, asking questions, and showing demos. 

💡 You can find the slides presented, recordings, and further references from the previous roundtables in this GitHub repository.


Meeting Information

When:                                                                      
  • March 18th, 10:00 - 11:00 AM CET  – Zoom Meeting (please register in advance) 
 
Who:
  • All interested customers, partners, and stakeholders are invited to join this roundtable
  • BTP ABAP team:
    • Frank Jentsch (Product Lead for SAP BTP ABAP Environment)
    • Iwona Hahn (BTP ABAP Product Management)
  • Partner rps GmbH:
    • Hans-Georg Lill, Martin Rau, Mark Lohmüller, Jerónimo Fernández Gersol 
Preliminary Agenda:
  • Product update for SAP BTP ABAP Environment
  • “rps Agreement Management” Partner Solution on SAP BTP incl. live demo
  • Q&A 
Looking forward to meeting you!
 


Check out our SAP Business Technology ABAP Environment page in SAP Community for product updates and upcoming events.

entry.title: SAP BTP ABAP Environment - Release 2502
entry.content:
SAP BTP ABAP Environment - Release 2502

Based on the new release 2502, this blog post provides an overview of the new features. The release has been available since February 15th and is the successor to the 2411 release. Some highlights of the 2502 release are the Downtime-optimized Upgrades and Hotfix Collection Imports for Production, the IDE Action Framework and the editable treeview in SAP Fiori Elements.

The complete list of all new features can be found on the What’s New for SAP BTP ABAP environment page.

 

❕This blog post does not deal with the new Joule for Developers, ABAP AI capabilities, which were also part of the release 2502. Information on this topic can be found in the following blog posts:

  • Joule speaks ABAP!
  • The Power of Joule for Developers, ABAP AI Capabilities
  • Unleash Your Inner ABAP Rockstar with Joule for developers , ABAP AI capabilities

 

ABAP Development Tools (ADT)

  • ABAP Memory Snapshot view to list all memory snapshots and from here it is also possible to open them in the editor (details)
  • Develop Custom Actions with the IDE Action Framework and Enhance Them with Server-Driven UI (for more information see IDE Actions and Working with Server-Driven UIs)
  • Updated ATC Check "Allowed Object Types in Cloud", it now only reports findings if the ABAP language version of the object is set to “Standard”

Core Data Services (CDS)

  • The new CDS table entity type is available
  • With CDS Writeable View Entities, you can wrap existing DDIC Tables (or CDS Table Entities) and expose a native CDS signature that you can use with ABAP SQL (SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE) in combination with DCL-based access control

Associations in RAP

  • Define nonstandard operations for associations, these are link action, unlink action and inverse function (details)

ABAP SQL Service

  • There is an increased feature scope for the ABAP SQL Service / ODBC Driver for ABAP to expose ABAP-based business data via SQL-APIs to external SQL consumers, besides exposing CDS view entities for read-only scenarios, it will be possible to also expose "read-only" AMDP-based database procedures to be used by external ODBC clients. By doing so, you can both expose SQL and SQLScript-based business logic to external ODBC clients

Event-Driven Side Effects

  • Define an event to trigger a side effect in your RAP business object, whenever a defined event is raised by the application server, the side effect is triggered and the defined targets are reloaded (details)

SAP Fiori Elements

  • In RAP, a treeview visualizes data modeled by a CDS hierarchy, using an SAP Fiori Elements treetable. Previously, you could model hierarchical structures and expose them as read-only treeviews to the SAP Fiori UI. Now you can also expose hierarchical structures as editable treeviews with draft capabilities (see Treeviews and Editable Treeviews with Draft Capabilities)

Application Jobs

  • New Methods for the Application Jobs API Class CL_APJ_RT_API, as the new method GET_JOB_PERIODICITY_INFO to read the periodicity of an application job. For more information on the new method, see the F2 help for this method in ABAP development tools for Eclipse. Similarly, the new methods GET_JOB_PARAM_VALUES and GET_TEMPLATE_PARAM_VALUES were introduced. You can retrieve the application job- and template parameter values with these methods

Units of Measure

  • It is now possible to maintain and delete translations for dimensions and units of measurement using released ABAP Cloud APIs (CL_UOM*) (details)

Changing HTTP Redirect Behavior

  • You can now change and the redirect behavior or retrieve the current redirect policy of an HTTP call using the SET_REDIRECT_POLICY and GET_REDIRECT_POLICY methods of the CL_WEB_HTTP_CLIENT_MANAGER class (details)

ABAP Integration & Connectivity

  • Developing Inbound and Outbound Communication, the documentation has been moved to a central guide dedicated to integration and connectivity (details)
  • Data Integration using SQL Service, the documentation Accessing ABAP-Managed Data Using SQL Services for Data Integration Scenarios has moved to a central guide dedicated to integration and connectivity (details)

Communication Management

  • Local APIs, the communication management will provide local APIs for the creation and update of communication arrangements, communication systems, communication users, and the upload of client certificates

Cipher Suites

  • Specify cipher suites in the Communication Systems app to define a set of encryption mechanisms you want to use as a basis for your communication (details)

Custom Business Configuration App

  • Title Name Change, when a business configuration object is selected, the application title now changes to the name of the business configuration object (details)

Transports

  • Open transport requests can be displayed by searching a table in the Manage Software Component app (details)
  • Export Customizing Transports, release all open customizing requests at once (details)

SAP Fiori Launchpad

  • Theme Selection according to OS Settings, this feature automatically detects the OS settings to adapt the themes (details)
  • Hide empty spaces and pages, there are not shown when empty because of the user’s role context

SAPUI5

  • Referenced SAPUI5 Version, As part of hotfix collection 4 (which is used for the standard upgrade), SAPUI5 version 1.130.6 is used for the administrator’s SAP Fiori launchpad, SAP-delivered Fiori apps, and the custom-built SAP Fiori apps, which are deployed to the ABAP system (see  SAP BTP ABAP Environment – Maintenance Windows and Major Upgrade Windows in 2025 and SAP UI5)

Key User Adaption

  • Harmonized Context Menu, is now consistent when selecting elements (details)
  • What’s new dialog, keeps you informed about the latest updates, features and improvements in key user adaption (details)

SAP HANA Cloud

  • Increased SAP HANA Cloud Backup Retention Time to 30 days instead of 14 days (details)

entry.title: SAP Developer News February 20th, 2025
entry.content:
DESCRIPTION

Podcast: https://podcast.opensap.info/sap-developers/2025/02/20/sap-developer-news-february-20th-2025/

 Joule for developers, ABAP AI capabilities is here!!!:

  • https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/joule-speaks-abap/ba-p/14018226
  • https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/the-power-of-joule-for-developers-abap-ai-capabilities/ba-p/14019716
  • https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/unleash-your-inner-abap-rockstar-with-joule-for-developers-abap-ai/ba-p/14019607
  • https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=OwtRsMRLPYU&list=PL6RpkC85SLQAt9lvPw0gF4E3nwbJD0EUe&index=1

ABAPconf YouTube channel content

  • Channel videos https://www.youtube.com/@abapconf/videos

Terraform and SAP BTP news

  • Getting Started with Terraform on SAP BTP https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/getting-started-with-terraform-on-sap-btp
  • New Release of the Terraform Exporter for SAP BTP – version 0.3.0-beta1 is out https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/new-release-terraform-exporter-sap-btp-version-out-christian-lechner-leraf/
  • Managing resources on SAP BTP – what tool do I choose? https://qmacro.org/blog/posts/2022/12/12/managing-resources-on-sap-btp-what-tool-do-i-choose/

Writable External Entities:

  • https://help.sap.com/docs/abap-cloud/abap-data-models/writable-external-entities?version=sap_btp

SAP Business Technology Platform Solution Diagram repository update

  • BTP Solution Diagram Repository https://sap.github.io/btp-solution-diagrams/

 

CHAPTER TITLES

0:00 Intro

0:10 Joule for developers, ABAP AI capabilities is here!!!

1:44 ABAPconf YouTube channel content

2:42 Terraform and SAP BTP news

4:01 Writable External Entities

5:02 SAP Business Technology Platform Solution Diagram repository update

 

TRANSCRIPTION

[Intro] This is the SAP developer News for February 20th, 2025.

[Richie] Hello, friends, Rich Heilman here, and of course the big news this week is around the much-anticipated release of Joule for developers' ABAP AI Capabilities. Yes, that's right, folks. The features that I've been showing you for the past couple years in the developer keynotes at SAP TechEd, these features are finally here. Our development teams have been working around the clock to bring you these new Joule capabilities to ABAP development tools in Eclipse or ADT. This week, we've seen several announcement blog posts from ABAP leadership, including Sonia, Alex, and Simona. So please check out these blog posts for more information about the first round of Joule capabilities for ABAP. Also, how you can get started using it today. And of course, more information on the long-term outlook. Also, I'll be publishing some videos, which were created by our development colleagues, showcasing all the great new features, and we'll be putting these videos out on the SAP developer's YouTube channel under a special playlist. The introduction is already out there. I have like 20 more to publish over the next few weeks, so stay tuned. For those who are looking to try this out on BTP ABAP trial, we will have to wait a little bit longer. We are currently looking for a viable solution to make that happen and I'll be sure to update you all on the progress. So stay tuned and bye for now.

[DJ] As you've no doubt heard this week, not least from my good friend and colleague Rich in this very same SAP developer news episode, Joule is now GA for ABAP. If you're an ABAP developer, this is a major milestone. but it's also a great opportunity to make sure your skills are up to date and your knowledge is fresh. Now, the ABAP CONF conference, a great friend and ally of the Code Connect week that takes place annually, has a YouTube channel with plenty of event session replays. Why not head over there and update your knowledge from experts in the field? Joule, of course, is great, but there are also some great gems over on this YouTube channel. See what I did that? I'll be learning.

[DJ] Beyond the SAP BTP cockpit, there are different ways to manage resources on the platform. There's the BTP CLI, of course, there's a family of APIs, and for open declarative management of resources, there's Terraform, and specifically the Terraform provider for SAP BTP. Now, the Terraform provider team have been working hard to create content for you to get started. And in fact, they've recently finished and published a course, getting started with Terraform on SAP BTP. It's around two to three hours, it's free and it's great content. Definitely recommended. Talking of Terraform on SAP BTP, the Terraform exporter for SAP BTP has just seen a new release published. Now, just to remind you, the Terraform exporter for SAP BTP is a way for you to get started with existing resources. We all have existing resources on SAP BTP, right? Anyway, for more details on this update and what it brings, check out Christian blog post.

[Shilpa] Hello, ABAP developers. You might know about CDS external entities, which allows ABAP applications to retrieve data from external system using SQL and make it available within the CDS infrastructure. Now, external system can be SAP HANA or non-SAP HANA data system or remote ASABAP. With the latest release of SAP BTP ABAP environment, 2502, these external entities can be defined as writable. Now, writable external entities can modify the data on the external systems using SQL operations like insert, update, modify, and delete. So in addition to the read-only scenarios, it is possible for the ABAP applications to modify the data on the external system using SQL. Now, writing is only possible via service connection in order to decouple the remote transaction from the local transaction. The syntax and more details is in the help document. I will leave the link in the description.

[Kevin] So one tool I regularly use is the SAP Business Technology Platform Solution Diagram repository. And for those who don't know what I'm talking about, the SAP BTP Solution Diagram repository is a collection of helpful icons, shapes, and other useful tools to easily create beautiful solution diagrams. And they regularly ship updates to that. And right now, we got a new update, allowing you to resize icons, which is great, and they also enhanced the SAP BTP foundational icons with updated SAP Cloud Identity Service icons and more. They added also document grounding to SAP BTP AI and extensibility service for BTP to SAP BTP AppDev Automation. And finally, they also added two new libraries. One is the SAP generic icons.

entry.title: “With ‘Joule for Developers’ We Have a Treasure in Our Hands”
entry.content:
Sonja Liénard, head of SAP BTP ABAP, has a long history in ABAP development and has held various roles at SAP. She has worked on the ABAP database interface, the ABAP Kernel, and ABAP Developer Tools.

Her teams are working all-out to ensure that the new capability in SAP Build released this week for general use by customers and partners – Joule for developers, ABAP AI capabilities (known internally as “Joule for ABAP”) – is continually tested and enhanced. In this Q&A, Sonja gives SAP News a look at the new capability and talks about its impact for both developers and SAP. 

SAP News: More than 7,000 ABAP developers at SAP have been testing the AI extensions for over three months now, along with 120 people from 13 pilot customers and partners. What does the support from Joule look like and what feedback have you received?

Sonja: The feedback has been very positive. Joule for developers can answer questions about programming, explain code, suggest code auto-completion, and perform unit tests. [See the box at the end of the article for explanations.]

Our more than 150 developers from a variety of departments who took part in our initial beta test rated satisfaction levels from 70% to more than 80% in all of these areas. The new AI features make their work much easier. As such, we are seeing very high recommendation rates.

The feedback from our customers and partners was even more positive: they are eager to start using the tools in their systems on an everyday basis. The many inquiries about additional use cases that we have received show us that our customers and partners are using Joule for developers intensively and have very high expectations of it.

Where isn’t the AI working satisfactorily yet? What needs to be improved?

We’re talking about an initial delivery – so it’s completely normal that we’re working on ongoing improvements. In particular, the predictive code completion was missing a key improvement during the test phase, but now has it. Our experts and data scientists worked all-out to achieve that. And the AI models are being trained on specific use cases so they can deliver the best results.

It’s also important to remember that Joule can’t read minds. The quality of the proposed code depends on how completely and accurately the developers specify their requirements. Without sufficient comments or specifications, the results will never be perfect – just like with other AI use cases.

We announced general availability of AI support for developers this week. Why is AI support for ABAP developers so important for SAP from a strategic perspective?

There are several reasons. First of all, because the market has been waiting for Joule for developers for quite a long time and there’s a lot of demand. It can significantly boost efficiency in development, along with user satisfaction.

Secondly, our model is unique because it’s based on millions of lines of ABAP code that only SAP has. So, we have something really valuable here! This model is truly one of a kind in this form.

Last but not least, Joule for developers will help SAP achieve its strategic goals, especially with regard to cloud strategy and increasing cloud revenue. A true ace up our sleeve, indeed.

What are the next steps? Is there anything else you’d like to say to our developers?

After general availability (GA), the next important step is the enhancement of the custom code migration tools with AI support. We’ve put together a dedicated team to concentrate on training the model for this migration, and it aims to truly transform from classic ABAP to ABAP Cloud.

This is a groundbreaking approach because it simplifies and accelerates custom code migration – that is, the transformation from classic ABAP, some of it developed or modified directly by our customers, into ABAP Cloud. This step supports RISE with SAP, for instance, as well as the general adoption of our cloud portfolio, and contributes directly to meeting SAP’s cloud revenue targets. It’s a real treasure!

And to the developers: if you’re not working with the tool yet, give it a try! We’re sure that it won’t just be a big help; it’ll be a lot of fun, too. Find out about innovations and info sessions. We’d be happy to get your feedback.
@lienards 
_____________________

This is how Joule for developers supports you: 

  • Chat lets developers interact directly with the tool, similar to Google and Copilot, to ask questions and get support for coding. 
  • Explain helps developers understand the existing code and development objects by giving them explanations of selected coding. 
  • Predictive code completion suggests lines of code for potential implementation, based on comments and the previously entered specifications. Developers can accept or reject this ghost text.
  • Unit test generation automates the generation of unit tests for ABAP classes and core data services and helps improve them, helping developers make sure that the code functions properly. 

    This interview was first published at SAP One, SAP's employee portal.

entry.title: How to build a responsive UI for a RAP business object that starts an asynchronous process
entry.content:
Introduction

Are you looking for a way to have responsive UI that starts an asynchronous process? 

With the background processing framework (BGPF) there is a way to start an asynchronous process from withing the RAP framework. This also allows to start processes that perform a commit work.

In this blog post I will show an easy way to start an asynchronous process using BGPF (the calculation of an inventory entry) and an easy way how to update the UI with the result of the calculation since with the latest release of the SAP BTP ABAP Environment a new feature was released which allows to refresh the UI of a SAP Fiori Elements applications by simply raising an event.   

 

Prerequisites

  • BGPF is available for private cloud and on premise SAP S/4HANA Systems as of release 2023 
  • Event Drive Side Effects are currently only available in SAP BTP ABAP Environment and SAP S/4HANA public cloud ABAP Environment. We plan to make this available for on premise and private cloud systems as of release 2025

Overview

A detailed introduction of the background processing can be found in the following blog post here Introducing the Background Processing Framework .

The background processing framework is the technology of choice when you want to start asynchronous processes in ABAP cloud rather than using application jobs which are meant to be used for long running and recurring processes. 

The generell problem when starting a long running process from within a stateless UI was that the UI could not be updated asynchronously unless you wanted to block the usage of the UI until the long running process has finished.

The source code of this sample class can be found in SAP-samples on Github.   

 Event driven side effects now allow to update the UI of a Fiori Elements application when an asynchronous process has finished. 

When working on the implementation a colleage of mine, @Martin_Müller , showed me an elegant way how a process can be started using BGPF by simply using a class that uses a class with a static method that takes the key of the updated entity of our RAP BO as a parameter in the save sequence of our RAP busines object.

The whole BGPF processing is done within this class and it thus makes the use of BGPF much easier and you end up with a simple call in an additional or unmanaged save in your behavior implementation class like the following:

 

 

 

DATA(bgpf_process_name) = zbgpfcl_calc_inventory_006=>run_via_bgpf( i_rap_bo_entity_key = update_inventory-%key ).

 

 

 

 

 

Implementation details

You can find the source code of a simple sample application in the following GitHub repository.

SAP-samples - How to use BGPF and events for side effects in ABAP RESTful programming model 

Further information

Event Drive Side Effects

  • SAP Online Help

Background Processing Framework

  • Blog Post: Introducing the Background Processing Framework 
  • SAP Online Help The RAP Transactional Model and the SAP LUW

entry.title: Be Part of Tomorrow: Join SAP's Customer Engagement Initiative Cycle 1, 2025!
entry.content:
We’re thrilled to announce the launch of the first cycle of the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative for 2025, featuring 50 exciting new projects aimed at fostering innovation and collaboration with our valued SAP customers and partners.

In today’s fast-paced business world, staying ahead is more important than ever. The SAP Customer Engagement Initiative presents a unique opportunity to gain early insights into SAP’s product developments. By participating, you’ll work directly with our developers and product managers, helping shape the future of our products.

Register until March 28, 2025! Don’t miss out on the opportunity to have your voice heard and influence the next generation of SAP solutions.

To help you explore the available projects, we’ve compiled a detailed list for your convenience. Check out the full list here or follow the links below to discover the exciting opportunities:

ABAP

  • ABAP Cloud Development Assistant
  • OData UI Service from scratch Generator

SAP Business Technology Platform

  • BTP AI based Data Anonymization Service
  • Create AI-driven extensions and automations with SAP Build
  • Improving the Admin's and Solution Architect's Experience on SAP BTP
  • SAP BPM, SAP BRM and SAP Process Orchestration migration to Cloud
  • SAP BTP Cockpit, Cloud Foundry UIs
  • SAP BTP Usability Event - User research project
  • SAP Business Technology Platform: Multi-Region Desaster Recovery
  • SAP Cloud Identity Services - Identity Provisioning, Integration with Joule 
  • SAP Integration Suite

SAP Concur

  • SAP Concur - AI Managed Expense Reports
  • SAP Concur - Dynamic Card Management – Virtual Credit Cards
  • SAP Concur Expense - Web Services for Financial Integration
  • SAP Concur Mobile app and the sign-in user experience
  • SAP Concur Solutions – All About E-Receipts
  • SAP Concur Solutions - Customer Managed Keys for granular control over data
  • SAP Concur Solutions - Sponsored Guest Feature
  • SAP Concur Solutions - Web Notifications Center and Preferences

SAP Customer Experience

  • Research project into a potential loyalty cloud solution
  • SAP CX AI Toolkit

Data & Analytics

  • Redesigning the Dashboard Experience
  • SAP HANA Cloud Elastic Compute Node
  • SAP HANA Database Migration to SAP HANA Cloud

SAP S/4HANA Cloud

  • Bank Relationship Management: Improving Overview, Services & Fee, and Interest Calculations
  • Fiori First for Sales Order Management
  • Identifying and resolving critical situations in a production planners day in S/4HANA Public Cloud
  • Identity and Access Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • Insights into Business processes with Business Event Logging
  • Joule and GenAI use cases in SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud
  • New Financial Reporting for Enterprise Project (Project Booklet) including ETO
  • Planned New Solution for Cost Component Breakdown
  • Project staffing, consider dependencies across different roles in a project
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition - Product Change Management
  • Simplification of transports in SAP S/4HANA® Cloud Public Edition, three-system landscape
  • Usability Improvements for managing attachments in S/4HANA business applications

SAP Globalization

  • Australia and New Zealand: GST requirement for Down payments
  • Australia: Modified Payment Times Report - AASB 8 and Taxable Payments Annual Report
  • ISO 21378 Audit Data Collection Standard
  • Planned enhancements to Run statutory reports to handle Draft Report reconciliation
  • Sales and Use Tax Reporting in United States - User Research
  • Sales and Use Tax payments in United States

Lifecycle Management

  • DevOps with ABAP

SAP Integration Experience Feedback goes SAP Customer Engagement Initiative

  • Assisted Assessment of Aligned Business APIs for External Workforce
  • Assisted Assessment of Aligned Business APIs for Hire to Retire Cloud
  • Assisted Assessment of Aligned Business APIs for Source-to-Pay

Others

  • Research into AI-Assisted User Learning Rollout for Adoption
  • SAP Reference Architecture in SAP LeanIX
  • Shape the future of SAP Cloud ALM Configuration & Security Analysis
  • The AI-Driven Future of New Product Introduction (NPI)

We believe that collaboration is key to sparking innovation. By working alongside our customers and partners, we can jointly shape the future of SAP’s products and transform business operations.

Let’s take this journey together. Together, we have the power to drive progress and transform industries.

Don’t miss out – sign up today at influence.sap.com and be a part of the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Cycle 1 in 2025!

Warm regards, 
Your SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Team 

Follow-up after registration: When you register for a project, you will be invited to an introductory call with the SAP project lead. At this point further participation is optional. Typically, all activities are governed by the Feedback Agreement with SAP.

The first few are out there now with a fun little introduction, and then we get right into the good stuff in the next episodes of the series, introducing you to Joule and how to get started using it today.
